title: 控方证人
summary:
After failing to find any news of a murder in the newspapers, Jack Hattersley becomes increasingly unsettled by a recurring auditory hallucination. While playing golf near a villa, he hears a woman’s voice screaming, "Murdered—help! Murdered!" at exactly 7:25 a.m. When he confronts the girl weeding in the villa's garden, she claims she heard nothing and suggests he may be suffering from shell shock. This interaction causes Jack to doubt his own sanity, fearing he is experiencing a delusion.

The following morning, Jack hears the same scream at 7:25 a.m. while running toward the golf course, confirming the sound originates from the direction of the villa. During a subsequent conversation with the girl, he feels she is patronizing him regarding his mental health. At breakfast, Jack notices Dr. Lavinton, a professional-looking man with piercing gray eyes, observing him intently, leading Jack to fear the doctor has detected his perceived mental instability.

To test whether the screams are real or a hallucination, Jack arranges to play golf with Mr. Ravelton the next morning. At 7:25 a.m., the scream occurs again while Ravelton is playing. Jack observes that Ravelton hears nothing, confirming that the sound is only audible to him. The shock of the event causes Jack to stumble and collapse on the grass, leaving him disoriented as Lavinton attempts to assist him.
